dimension_scores.reason: email-env__send_email sent a farewell email to ryan.oconnor@amazon.com that explicitly thanked him for 'more than a decade,' mentioned final pay follow-up, and mentioned COBRA/benefits follow-up in a warm professional tone.

dimension_scores.dimension: Exit Interview Scheduling & NPC Preferences (Nancy Cooper)
dimension_scores.reason: The agent first DM'd Nancy for topics, received her requested items via rocketchat history, and then chronos-server__create_event created a neutral 'Exit Interview — Ryan O'Connor' event whose description clearly included Q1 vendor contract renewals handoff and logistics tracking platform credential revocation plus knowledge transfer.

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm shows a supportive DM to Nancy acknowledging her frustration and confirming offboarding steps are underway, and a separate DM to Catherine asking which Finance contact to CC before the final pay email.

dimension_scores.dimension: Final Pay Coordination Email & NPC Preferences (Catherine Moore)
dimension_scores.reason: After Catherine replied naming Thomas Reed, email-env__send_email sent the final pay coordination email to Catherine with CC to Thomas and actionable items, though it included extra benefits/pension language and employee details beyond the minimum needed.

frappe-hrms-env__frappe_update_resource changed EMP-023 status from Active to Left, and frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource created Note SIM-BC3148CE documenting the offboarding in professional language.
Most actions matched the task, but creating an HRIS Note was an additional state-changing action not explicitly requested, while the rest of the workflow stayed in scope.

evidence: RocketChat channel history showed Nancy's frustrated #ops-transitions message, which the agent referenced before contacting her.
rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm to nancy_cooper acknowledged the tough timing during Q1 planning, confirmed active handling of offboarding, and asked what to include in the exit interview.

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_get_room_history for Nancy captured her requested agenda topics: Q1 vendor contract renewals handoff, logistics tracking platform access credential revocation, and knowledge transfer wrap-up.
rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm to catherine_moore asked which Finance contact should be CC'd before sending the final pay email.
rocketchat-env__rocketchat_get_room_history for Catherine showed her instruction to CC Thomas Reed and keep her included.
email-env__send_email successfully sent the farewell email to ryan.oconnor@amazon.com with the subject 'Farewell and Next Steps — Ryan O'Connor'.
email-env__send_email successfully sent the final pay coordination email to catherine.moore@amazon.com and the body/tool arguments show cc: thomas.reed@amazon.com.
chronos-server__list_events found both Nancy and Ryan calendars clear for the week, and chronos-server__create_event created a 30-minute event on 2025-01-22 from 10:00 to 10:30 with both attendees.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_update_resource returned Ryan's employee record with status changed to 'Left'.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource created HRIS Note SIM-BC3148CE documenting the offboarding.
failed_criteria: Final pay email may be slightly over-scoped by including benefits/pension language and extra employee details not strictly necessary.
One extra state-changing action occurred: creation of an HRIS Note was not explicitly required by the prompt.
notes: The farewell email followed the prompt's 'over a decade' framing despite HRIS showing a 2021 join date; this matches the task request but reflects a data inconsistency.
If strict minimal-scope grading is applied, the extra HRIS note should be the only notable deduction.

artifacts.task: Ryan O'Connor is resigning effective immediately after over a decade in Operations. Handle his offboarding: send him a farewell email acknowledging his tenure and mentioning HR will follow up on final pay and COBRA/benefits details, and schedule a 30-minute exit interview with him and Nancy Cooper this week. Nancy posted a frustrated message in the #ops-transitions channel — send her a Chat DM acknowledging the situation and confirming offboarding is underway. Before emailing Catherine Moore about coordinating final pay, reach out to Catherine via Chat to confirm which specific Finance contact should be copied on the final pay coordination email — she may want someone from her team looped in. Also, check with Nancy Cooper via Chat for any specific items she wants covered during the exit interview so you can include them in the calendar event description.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Ryan O'Connor is resigning effective immediately after over a decade in Operations. Handle his offboarding: send him a farewell email acknowledging his tenure and mentioning HR will follow up on final pay and COBRA/benefits details, and schedule a 30-minute exit interview with him and Nancy Cooper this week. Nancy posted a frustrated message in the #ops-transitions channel — send her a Chat DM acknowledging the situation and confirming offboarding is underway. Before emailing Catherine Moore about coordinating final pay, reach out to Catherine via Chat to confirm which specific Finance contact should be copied on the final pay coordination email — she may want someone from her team looped in. Also, check with Nancy Cooper via Chat for any specific items she wants covered during the exit interview so you can include them in the calendar event description.
I'll handle Ryan O'Connor's offboarding systematically. Let me start with Phase 1: Research.

function.arguments: {"to_username": "nancy_cooper", "text": "Hi Nancy, I saw your message in #ops-transitions and completely understand your frustration \u2014 losing Ryan with no transition time during Q1 planning is a tough situation. I want you to know that I'm actively handling his offboarding right now: updating his HRIS record, sending his farewell email, and coordinating final pay with Finance.\n\nI'm also scheduling a 30-minute exit interview with you and Ryan this week. Before I set that up, are there any specific items or topics you'd like covered during the exit interview? I want to make sure the agenda reflects your priorities. Please let me know and I'll include them in the invite."}

Before I do, I wanted to check with you \u2014 is there a specific Finance contact from your team you'd like me to CC on that final pay coordination email? I want to make sure the right person is looped in from the start."}

function.arguments:
{"to": "ryan.oconnor@amazon.com", "subject": "Farewell and Next Steps — Ryan O'Connor", "body": "Dear Ryan,

I'm writing to acknowledge your resignation, effective immediately. On behalf of the entire team, I want to sincerely thank you for your years of dedicated service in Operations. Over more than a decade with us, you've made meaningful contributions that have shaped the department, and your expertise and commitment will be greatly missed.

As part of your offboarding, please be aware of the following next steps:

• Final Pay: HR is coordinating with Finance to ensure your final paycheck — including any accrued PTO and outstanding compensation — is processed promptly. You'll receive details on timing and method shortly.

• COBRA / Benefits: You will receive information regarding your COBRA eligibility and options for continuing your health benefits coverage. HR will follow up with the specific enrollment details, deadlines, and any other benefits-related matters.

• Exit Interview: We've scheduled a 30-minute exit interview with you and Nancy Cooper this week. You'll receive a calendar invite shortly.

If you have any questions in the meantime, please don't hesitate to reach out to me directly.

Wishing you all the best in your next chapter, Ryan. Thank you for everything.

Warm regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"to": "catherine.moore@amazon.com", "cc": "thomas.reed@amazon.com", "subject": "Final Pay Coordination — Ryan O'Connor Offboarding", "body": "Dear Catherine,

I'm reaching out to coordinate the final pay process for Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023, Operations Analyst), who has resigned effective immediately.

Could you and Thomas please assist with the following:

1. Final Paycheck: Processing Ryan's final paycheck, including any accrued but unused PTO, prorated salary, and any outstanding reimbursements.

2. Benefits Closeout: Coordination on pension/clearance steps and any benefits-related financial items that need to be settled.

3. Timeline: Given the immediate nature of the resignation, we'd like to ensure the final pay is processed as quickly as possible in compliance with applicable regulations.

Ryan's details for reference:
• Employee ID: EMP-023
• Department: Operations
• Designation: Operations Analyst
• Cost Center: CC-OPS-023

Please let me know if you need any additional information or documentation from HR to proceed. Thank you for your prompt attention to this.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"doctype": "Note", "payload": {"title": "Offboarding — Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023)", "content": "Employee: Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023)
Department: Operations
Designation: Operations Analyst
Reports To: Christopher Taylor (Operations Lead)

Action: Immediate Resignation & Offboarding
Effective Date: Immediate

Summary:
Ryan O'Connor has resigned effective immediately. The following offboarding steps have been completed:

1. HRIS Status: Updated to 'Left'
2. Farewell Email: Sent to Ryan acknowledging his tenure, with information about final pay and COBRA/benefits follow-up.
3. Final Pay Coordination: Email sent to Catherine Moore (CFO) with CC to Thomas Reed (Finance Manager) to process final paycheck, accrued PTO, and benefits closeout.
4. Exit Interview: Scheduled for January 22, 2025, 10:00–10:30 AM with Nancy Cooper (Operations Director). Agenda includes Q1 vendor contract renewals handoff, logistics platform access credential revocation, and knowledge transfer.
5. Manager Notification: Nancy Cooper (Operations Director) has been informed and consulted on exit interview agenda items.

Next Steps:

input.text:
Hi Nancy, I saw your message in #ops-transitions and completely understand your frustration — losing Ryan with no transition time during Q1 planning is a tough situation. I want you to know that I'm actively handling his offboarding right now: updating his HRIS record, sending his farewell email, and coordinating final pay with Finance.

I'm also scheduling a 30-minute exit interview with you and Ryan this week. Before I set that up, are there any specific items or topics you'd like covered during the exit interview? I want to make sure the agenda reflects your priorities. Please let me know and I'll include them in the invite.

Hi Catherine, I'm processing the offboarding for Ryan O'Connor (Operations Analyst, EMP-023) who is resigning effective immediately. I need to send you a formal email about coordinating his final pay and benefits. Before I do, I wanted to check with you — is there a specific Finance contact from your team you'd like me to CC on that final pay coordination email? I want to make sure the right person is looped in from the start.

input.subject: Farewell and Next Steps — Ryan O'Connor
input.body:
Dear Ryan,

I'm writing to acknowledge your resignation, effective immediately. On behalf of the entire team, I want to sincerely thank you for your years of dedicated service in Operations. Over more than a decade with us, you've made meaningful contributions that have shaped the department, and your expertise and commitment will be greatly missed.

As part of your offboarding, please be aware of the following next steps:

• Final Pay: HR is coordinating with Finance to ensure your final paycheck — including any accrued PTO and outstanding compensation — is processed promptly. You'll receive details on timing and method shortly.

• COBRA / Benefits: You will receive information regarding your COBRA eligibility and options for continuing your health benefits coverage. HR will follow up with the specific enrollment details, deadlines, and any other benefits-related matters.

• Exit Interview: We've scheduled a 30-minute exit interview with you and Nancy Cooper this week. You'll receive a calendar invite shortly.

If you have any questions in the meantime, please don't hesitate to reach out to me directly.

Wishing you all the best in your next chapter, Ryan. Thank you for everything.

Warm regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Final Pay Coordination — Ryan O'Connor Offboarding
input.body:
Dear Catherine,

I'm reaching out to coordinate the final pay process for Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023, Operations Analyst), who has resigned effective immediately.

Could you and Thomas please assist with the following:

1. Final Paycheck: Processing Ryan's final paycheck, including any accrued but unused PTO, prorated salary, and any outstanding reimbursements.

2. Benefits Closeout: Coordination on pension/clearance steps and any benefits-related financial items that need to be settled.

3. Timeline: Given the immediate nature of the resignation, we'd like to ensure the final pay is processed as quickly as possible in compliance with applicable regulations.

Ryan's details for reference:
• Employee ID: EMP-023
• Department: Operations
• Designation: Operations Analyst
• Cost Center: CC-OPS-023

Please let me know if you need any additional information or documentation from HR to proceed. Thank you for your prompt attention to this.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.description:
30-minute exit interview for Ryan O'Connor's departure from Operations.

Agenda:
1) Handoff status on the Q1 vendor contract renewals Ryan was managing
   - Current outstanding items, key contacts, timelines, and any blockers
   - Status of documents, approvals required, and next steps for a smooth transition

2) Access credentials for the logistics tracking platform
   - List of all access rights no longer needed, with rationale for revocation
   - Timeline and method for revoking credentials, plus confirmation of successful revocation
   - Any dependencies or affected workflows to watch during the revocation process

3) Knowledge transfer and successor handoff wrap-up

Attendees: Ryan O'Connor, Nancy Cooper, HR

content.text: Now let me create the HRIS Note documenting the offboarding.

payload.title: Offboarding — Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023)
payload.content:
Employee: Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023)
Department: Operations
Designation: Operations Analyst
Reports To: Christopher Taylor (Operations Lead)

Action: Immediate Resignation & Offboarding
Effective Date: Immediate

Summary:
Ryan O'Connor has resigned effective immediately. The following offboarding steps have been completed:

1. HRIS Status: Updated to 'Left'
2. Farewell Email: Sent to Ryan acknowledging his tenure, with information about final pay and COBRA/benefits follow-up.
3. Final Pay Coordination: Email sent to Catherine Moore (CFO) with CC to Thomas Reed (Finance Manager) to process final paycheck, accrued PTO, and benefits closeout.
4. Exit Interview: Scheduled for January 22, 2025, 10:00–10:30 AM with Nancy Cooper (Operations Director). Agenda includes Q1 vendor contract renewals handoff, logistics platform access credential revocation, and knowledge transfer.
5. Manager Notification: Nancy Cooper (Operations Director) has been informed and consulted on exit interview agenda items.

Next Steps:
- Complete exit interview on 1/22/2025
- Finalize pay and benefits processing with Finance
- Revoke system access and credentials
- Complete knowledge transfer documentation
